scenario: useState vs useRef for non-rendering state.
context: Need to track value but no re-render needed.
sam_initial_position: useState if value affects render later.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For internal tracking (last scroll position), useRef.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Pattern: useRef for truly internal, useState for renderable.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors useState for everything. Senior insight: useRef for non-render state.

context: Component wraps another, parent needs ref.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Components that wrap inputs/buttons should forward refs.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React 19+: ref as prop. React 18-: forwardRef.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't forward refs. Senior insight: wrappers without ref forwarding break common patterns.

scenario: Form layout: vertical vs horizontal vs grid.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Grid on desktop saves vertical space.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Mobile-first vertical, expand to grid on md+.

code_examples.code:
<form className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  <Field name="firstName" label="First name" />
  <Field name="lastName" label="Last name" />
  <Field name="email" label="Email" className="md:col-span-2" />
  <Field name="address" label="Address" className="md:col-span-2" />
  <Field name="city" label="City" />
  <Field name="zip" label="ZIP" />
</form>

what_juniors_miss: Juniors only vertical. Senior insight: forms benefit from grid layouts on desktop.

scenario: Initial data: SSR injection vs client fetch.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: SSR shows data on first paint. No skeleton flash.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors client fetch primary. Senior insight: SSR primary content for SEO and LCP.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For genuine one-off (specific design need), OK.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Lint rule to flag arbitrary values, comment to override.

context_dependent_answer: Lint warn on arbitrary, override with comment.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors arbitrary values everywhere. Senior insight: design system drift starts with arbitrary.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: FLIP is the canonical technique. ~30 LOC.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For one component: Framer. For general: FLIP.

context_dependent_answer: Framer for app code. FLIP for libs.
decision_framework: Framer for apps. FLIP for libs.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors no animation on reorder. Senior insight: FLIP (via library) makes reordering smooth.

scenario: Stable test selectors: data-testid vs role/name.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Copy changes should update tests. Real signal.

context_dependent_answer: Role/name. testid only when name unstable.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors data-testid everything. Senior insight: role/name test accessibility AND functionality.

scenario: Asserting types: assertion functions vs type guards.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both have place. Asserts for top-of-function. Guards in conditionals.

context_dependent_answer: Asserts for invariants. Guards in conditionals.
decision_framework: Asserts for invariants, guards for conditionals.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't use asserts. Senior insight: assertion functions narrow without ceremony.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: preconnect does DNS + TCP + TLS. Bigger win.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: preconnect has cost if origin unused.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. preconnect for fonts, prefetch for analytics.

code_examples.code:
<head>
  <!-- Critical third-party: preconnect (DNS + TCP + TLS) -->
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
  
  <!-- Less critical: dns-prefetch only -->
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://analytics.example.com" />
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://cdn.example.com" />
  
  <!-- Critical resource: preload -->
  <link rel="preload" href="/main.woff2" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin />
</head>

what_juniors_miss: Juniors no hints. Senior insight: third-party origins benefit from connection warming.

scenario: Sourcemaps in dev: inline vs separate file.
context_dependent_answer: Vite default (inline for cheap, separate for full).
what_juniors_miss: Juniors disable sourcemaps. Senior insight: sourcemaps are essential for debugging.

scenario: Component variants: cva vs custom function.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: cva handles variants, compound variants, defaults.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors ternary class strings. Senior insight: cva is the variant pattern.

sam_initial_position: Use React Query mutation - it captures.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: React Query mutation onError catches it.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Use RQ when applicable. try/catch fallback.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors fire-and-forget mutations. Senior insight: RQ mutations have proper error handling.

alex_initial_position: Granular vendor chunks per major dep.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granular allows cache stability per dep update.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors single vendor. Senior insight: cache stability via grouping.

scenario: Skip to main content link target.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: <main> jump positions cursor at top of main.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Some SR users want first interactive.

code_examples.code: Skip to main content

what_juniors_miss: Juniors no target. Senior insight: skip link needs target with tabindex=-1 to be focusable.

scenario: Public vs private API conventions in TypeScript.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: _ prefix is convention. Linter can enforce.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For class private fields, # is real private.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors public everything. Senior insight: real privacy via # and modules.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React 18 auto-batches even in event handlers, promises, setTimeout.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: flushSync for cases where you need immediate (e.g., scroll into view).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: flushSync rare. Most cases trust batching.

context_dependent_answer: Trust auto-batch. flushSync for immediate sync.
decision_framework: Auto-batch default. flushSync for sync DOM needs.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors flushSync everywhere. Senior insight: auto-batch is correct default.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For libraries with components that take render functions (DataGrid renderCell), still useful.

context_dependent_answer: Hooks default. Render props in library APIs.
decision_framework: Hooks default. Render props for library APIs needing per-item render.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors avoid render props. Senior insight: still useful in library API contracts.

context: Choosing RHF locks you into RHF idioms.
alex_initial_position: Accept lock-in. RHF is industry standard.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Wrappers hide RHF features. Bad abstractions.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Use RHF idioms directly + ESLint for consistency.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors over-abstract. Senior insight: idiomatic use is clearest.

scenario: GraphQL: client cache vs server cache.
alex_initial_position: Both. Server caches reduce backend load. Client caches UI fast.
sam_initial_position: Client only. Server caching is server concern.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Multi-user caching at edge (CDN) reduces server compute.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: GraphQL queries differ per user. Hard to CDN.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For public queries, persisted query + CDN.

context_dependent_answer: Client cache + persisted public queries on CDN.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't cache GraphQL. Senior insight: persisted queries enable CDN caching.

alex_initial_position: Native CSS. Modern browsers support most.
sam_initial_position: PostCSS for compat (nesting, container queries fallback).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Native CSS nesting, has() in all major browsers.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For older browsers, PostCSS. Modern: native.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors still use Sass. Senior insight: native CSS is mostly enough now.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For animation: cleanup. For network: AbortController.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't cancel. Senior insight: animations leak without cleanup.

alex_initial_position: MSW for network. vi.mock for module.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mock fetch globally. Senior insight: layer-appropriate mocking is cleaner.

alex_initial_position: Type-state builder. Each method returns new type.
sam_initial_position: Simple builder. All methods return self.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Type-state prevents calling methods out of order.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For library APIs, type-state worth it.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors fluent without types. Senior insight: type-state encodes call order.

scenario: Cold start optimization: edge function vs Node server.
alex_initial_position: Edge runtime (Cloudflare Workers). Fast cold start.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For SSR + simple API, edge fits.

context_dependent_answer: Edge for SSR/API. Node for heavy compute.
decision_framework: Edge for IO-bound. Node for compute-bound.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors deploy Node everywhere. Senior insight: edge runtime fits IO-bound workloads.

code_examples.code:
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4
  - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
    with:
      version: 9
  - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    with:
      node-version: 20
      cache: 'pnpm'
  - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  - run: pnpm test

what_juniors_miss: Juniors install fresh always. Senior insight: cached deps dramatically speed CI.
